---
title: "Strategic Demographics"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 

Background Review
According to the MOH there are approximately 148 hospitals with a FAC. Strategic plans were identified in 124 of these.
Seven to the original 148  are no longer reporting data, In all cases this means the hospital has merged with another entity, of the remaining a small number are private corporations, which do not post plans. A small number of hosptials do not post plans or indicated the plans were not yet available and in at least one case the Plan was not parsable. 
All strategic plans were read by an AI, which provided detailed information: 1) the dates covered by the plan, 2 Each strategic direction, 2) summary text describing the direction, and 3 Action steps 
A strict extraction only protocol was used to direct the AI,the AI could only use exact text from the strategic directions and text from elsewhere in the document,Mission, vision,Values was not permitted.
Extensive audits (25%) of the data was done and text was always presented accurately. 

There is variation in how hosptials present there strategy.  In four of the hospitals, the Strategic plan only directions, No descriptive text about the description and no documented actions. So for 120 hospitals there is either descriptive text or actions, and most hospitals (77) provided both text and specific actions. 






```{r}
 RawData<-readRDS("E:/HospitalStrategyGemini/Output/RawStrategyFeb2026.rds") %>%
  select(FAC) %>% unique()
nrow(RawData)

FullData<-readRDS("E:/HospitalStrategyGemini/Output/StrategyFeb2026.rds")


UniqueFAC<-FullData %>%
  select(FAC) %>% unique()
nrow(UniqueFAC)
NoTextNoActions<-FullData %>%
 filter(Descriptive_text!="NF",Actions!="NF") %>%
  select(FAC,Hospital_Name) %>% unique()
ByType<-FullData %>%
  select(FAC,Type) %>% unique() %>%
  group_by(Type) %>%
  mutate(CountType=n())%>% select(Type,CountType) %>%unique
tail(FullData,10)
```

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Ctrl+Alt+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Ctrl+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
